markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: **GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 release date anchors trader sentiment in the "What will happen before GTA VI?" market, where near-even odds reflect tight timelines and cultural speculation.** With Rockstar and Take-Two locking the date after prior delays and signaling summer marketing ramp-up, attention turns to high-volume outcomes like a new Playboi Carti album, Rihanna project, GPT-6 launch, or shifts in the Trump presidency, each hovering around 51-53% implied probability. Industry dynamics amplify this as publishers shuffle fall 2026 schedules to avoid the cultural juggernaut, while traders weigh unconfirmed music rollouts, AI timelines, and political events against the fixed five-month window. Upcoming catalysts include summer 2026 announcements and any late-year chart or tech breakthroughs that could tip specific resolutions before the November lock.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial on May 15, 2026, stands as the dominant driver behind trader consensus.** After a hung jury on the Jessica Mann rape charge, prosecutors have until mid-June to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial, injecting fresh uncertainty into an already protracted legal saga. Weinstein’s advanced age (74), documented health issues including cancer and diabetes, and ongoing appeal of his 2022 California conviction (16-year consecutive sentence) further tilt implied probabilities heavily toward limited or no additional prison time. Historical patterns of hung juries and prosecutorial fatigue in high-profile cases, combined with the “skin-in-the-game” assessment of outcomes already served since 2020, reinforce the market’s view that substantial new sentences remain unlikely in the near term. Upcoming decisions on retrial and any plea discussions represent the next clear catalysts.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain lead the 2026 World Cup winner market at implied probabilities of 18.4% and 14.1% due to deep squads, recent major-tournament pedigree, and favorable early fixtures amid the expanded 48-team group stage. Spain’s Euro 2024 title and attacking talent around Lamine Yamal have kept them competitive despite a draw with Cape Verde, while France benefits from Kylian Mbappé’s presence and squad versatility under Didier Deschamps. England, Portugal, and defending champions Argentina sit close behind, supported by strong FIFA rankings and club form, but face tougher paths and injury concerns. Multiple contenders in the 10-18% range reflect balanced depth across Europe and South America, with group results and fitness updates likely to drive further shifts in trader consensus.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of any designated successor or visible internal challenges. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling his precedent-breaking third term in 2022, and analyses indicate positioning for a fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit and planned engagements, underscores his active role without signs of health constraints or elite opposition. Potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, reinforcing continuity. This political stability underpins trader consensus reflected in the 93.3% implied probability on "No."

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 24.3% due to his sustained national profile as California governor, including recent media appearances, a memoir rollout, AI regulatory moves, and high-visibility positioning against the Trump administration.
